subparts.text: What is phishing, and how can users protect themselves from phishing attacks? Explain how information theft occurs. What measures can be taken to prevent it? 1
Encrypt the plaintext "HELLO" using the Playfair cipher with the key "MONARCHY". 1
What is the value of ϕ ? What are the number of elements in Z14*? 2
Using the Fermat’s Little Theorem find the result of 312 mod 11. Also, find the result of 3-1 mod 7. 2
Differentiate between active and passive attacks with suitable examples. Explain how each type affects cybersecurity. 1
Explain the working of a Hill cipher and encrypt the plaintext "ATTACK” using the key matrix: [2 3 3 6] . 1
Discuss the differences between encryption and hashing on different aspects. Give examples on each of the symmetric encryption, asymmetric encryption, and a hash function. 1
Using the Chinese Remainder Theorem, find the solution to the simultaneous equations: x ≡ 2 mod 3 x ≡ 3 mod 5 x ≡ 2 mod 7 2
How does the Diffie-Hellman Key Exchange securely establish a shared secret over an insecure channel? Illustrate with a step-by- step process. 2
Explain the importance of key distribution and management in cryptographic security. Discuss the Key management life cycle. What challenges arise in secure key distribution? 2
